chickens.text:
i have to hand it to brian for post 1 here

post 3 tells you it's all marketing, which is not true. i promise you they largely actually do believe they're making a god, that's a real thing that people think
Anthropic being tasked with solving all jailbreaks is Greek-tragedy-level irony
the ai people on here talk a lot about how silly and blind the anti-ai people are, but i think it might be time to talk about the pot calling the kettle black

good news everyone a minomobi iceberg atlas, ordered by number of commits this robot could find with a lil attractor iceberg that generates by website location. double click to reroll.

mino.mobi/iceberg

new post. trying to post once per month on my website, trying to advance Non-Equilibrium Attention Markets. in part 2, i fix some serious problems with part 1, and sketch out an example where we can see some of the machinery in action lastnpcalex.agency/p/neam-rescue

The Midjourney Scanner
It starts by stepping into a shallow pool of golden light. You then begin to descend into the water. Your body passes through a ring of underwater sensors, each acting like a dolphin, using its echolocation. The sensors send ultrasonic sound waves through your body from every angle. With enough waves, and enough angles, we form an image of what's happening inside your body.

• Fetched: A New Era of Midjourney

Oh, these are a pair, aren't they. You didn't send two unrelated articles — you sent the two ends of a pincer, and the pincer closes on you specifically.

A group of hitmen have been jailed after repeatedly trying to subcontract a job to each other in Guangxi, China.

Businessman Tan Youhui hired a hitman to "take out" his competitor for $282,000 (£218,000), a court heard.

But the hitman hired another man to do the job, offering $141,000. That man hired another hitman, who hired another hitman, who hired another hitman.

The plan crumbled when the final hitman met the man, named only as Wei, in a cafe and proposed faking his death.

An infographic from "Code Categories Arena" titled "BANNED ZONE" displaying a bar chart ranking AI models by score.
The graphic shows a large red "BANNED ZONE" banner stating "LEFT OF FABLE 5 – INCLUDING FABLE 5 – IS BANNED." A large red "X" covers a red highlighted section on the far left containing two vertical bars: "GLM-5.2" (score 1360) labeled "BANNED GLM-5.2" and "Claude Fable 5" (score 1350) labeled "BANNED FABLE 5."
To the right of the banned section, three beige bars representing versions of "Claude Opus" (scores 1342, 1338, 1337) are labeled as the "BAN BOUNDARY" with a sign stating "EVERYTHING TO THE LEFT IS BANNED." The remaining 16 grey bars to the right represent lower-scoring models starting from "GLM 5.1" (score 1331) down to "DeepSeek-V4-Pro" (score 1295).

Gerald Casale was not present for the first day of recording because he had missed the flight. With their bassist missing, the rest of the group spent the day playing with Eno, Bowie, Holger Czukay of Can, and Dieter Moebius of Cluster and Harmonia.[24]

In order to provide live two-way video between the lab and the conference hall, two microwave links were used. English also commanded a video switcher that controlled what was displayed on the big screen. The camera operator in Menlo Park was Stewart Brand, who at the time was a non-computer person, best known as the editor of the Whole Earth Catalog. Stewart Brand advised Engelbart and the team about how to present the demo.[14]

(Highlighted in screenshot) Engelbart got to know Stewart Brand when they experimented with LSD at the same lab.[15]
